Title: President vs. Pope: How feud with Leo could hurt Trump
Article:
US President Donald Trump has feuded with Pope Leo XIV over the Iran war – setting off an unholy row that could have serious political implications for the Republican leader at home.
Trump has drawn barbs even from some allies over the attacks on the US-born pontiff, who has criticised the Trump administration over its immigration crackdown, the intervention in Venezuela and the Iran war.
The president risks alienating the religious right in November's crucial US midterm elections.
So far the unprecedented clash between the leader of the most powerful military on Earth and the head of the world's 1.4 billion Catholics shows no signs of abating.
"There's nothing to apologise for. He's wrong," the 79-year-old Trump told reporters at the White House on Monday.
In the post on Sunday, Trump called the pontiff "WEAK on Crime, and terrible for Foreign Policy," suggesting that Leo was elected pope in May 2025 only because he was American and a possible bridge to the Trump administration.
Trump then posted an AI-generated image seemingly depicting himself as a figure like Jesus Christ, which he later deleted. He insisted on Monday that he believed the image showed himself as a doctor.
For his part, Pope Leo told reporters on the papal plane en route to Africa earlier Monday that he has "no fear, neither of the Trump administration nor speaking out loudly about the message of the Gospel."
Leo had earlier this month branded Trump's threat to destroy a "whole civilisation" in Iran as "truly unacceptable." He has also previously criticised Trump's mass deportation campaign as "inhuman."
Three-times married billionaire Trump has long reached out to America's evangelical Christians with his conservative, nativist vision.
They backed him in his election wins in 2016 and 2024 despite a series of scandals and an ambiguous personal relationship with religion.
But Trump, who has previously hawked US$60 Bibles branded with his name, appeared to have had something of an awakening during his second term.
At his inauguration last year he said he had been "saved by God" after a 2024 assassination attempt on the campaign trail and has taken a more explicitly religious tone.
[[nid:1417490]]
Yet over the recent Easter period, which is sacred to Christians, Trump has made a series of eye-opening posts when it comes to religion.
On the morning of Easter Sunday, as Christians were celebrating around the world, Trump posted a profanity-laced warning to the "crazy bastards" of Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz or else – bizarrely signing off: "Praise be to Allah."
Then, amid what appeared to be increasing frustration after talks with Iran produced no breakthrough, came Sunday's attacks on Pope Leo.
"I am disheartened that the president chose to write such disparaging words about the Holy Father," the head of the US Conference of Catholic Bishops, Archbishop Paul Coakley, said in a statement.
At least one prominent Catholic in Trump's administration backed the US president over the pontiff.
US Vice President JD Vance, a recent convert, told Fox News on Monday, "in some cases, it would be best for the Vatican to stick to matters of morality... and let the President United States stick to dictating American public policy."
There was no immediate reaction from Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who is also Catholic.
Perhaps more worrying for the White House is the ire on the religious right, particularly among former allies.
Any slackening of support for Trump will add to concerns among Republicans that they could lose control of Congress in November's mid-term elections, with the economy already a worry amid high oil prices caused by the Iran war.
"On Orthodox Easter, President Trump attacked the Pope because the Pope is rightly against Trump's war in Iran and then he posted this picture of himself as if he is replacing Jesus," one time ally and former congresswoman Marjorie Taylor-Greene said.
"This comes after last week's post of his evil tirade on Easter and then threatening to kill an entire civilisation. I completely denounce this and I'm praying against it!!!"
Conservative commentator Riley Gaines also railed against the apparent Jesus image.
"Seriously, I cannot understand why he'd post this," Gaines said on X, urging Trump to show humility and adding: "God shall not be mocked."
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KTMB offers 30pct weekday train fare discount from April to October
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd (KTMB) is offering a 30 per cent discount on train tickets across all its sectors from tomorrow until April 20.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said that, in addition to Electric Train Service (ETS) tickets, the discount for travel between April 15 and Oct 14 will also apply to passengers of the Ekspres Rakyat Timuran (ERT) for the JB Sentral–Tumpat–JB Sentral route.
He said the initiative is offered for travel from Monday to Thursday as part of the government's ongoing efforts through the Transport Ministry and KTMB.
"It is aimed at encouraging the use of public transport through more comfortable, safe and efficient rail services for the people," he said.
Loke said this at a press conference in conjunction with the Rail Usage Campaign to Address Rising Fuel Costs at the ministry here today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud: court
Article:
BEIJING: The founder of Chinese property giant Evergrande has pleaded guilty to charges of fraud and bribery, a court said on Tuesday, in the latest blow for what was once the country's leading developer.
Evergrande's rise was propelled by decades of rapid urbanisation and rising living standards, but in 2020, its access to credit dramatically narrowed when the government introduced curbs on excessive borrowing and speculation.
The company defaulted in 2021 after struggling to repay creditors.
Founder Xu Jiayin, known as Hui Ka Yan in Cantonese, was reportedly held by police in 2023, with Evergrande saying he had been subjected to measures "due to suspicion of illegal crimes."
A public hearing was held Monday and Tuesday on a case against Xu for "illegally absorbing public deposits, fundraising fraud, illegally issuing loans, illegally using funds, fraudulently issuing securities, disclosing important information in violation of regulations, embezzlement and (corporate) bribery", a court statement said.
"Xu Jiayin pleaded guilty and expressed remorse in court," the statement from the Shenzhen Intermediate People's Court in southern Guangdong province said, without elaborating further.
Evergrande Group and its real estate arm also stood trial.
The court said it would announce a verdict at a later date.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bukit Aman internal security unit roped into Port Klang e-waste task force
Article:
PORT KLANG: The Port Klang Joint Task Force on e-waste has agreed to bring in the police's Internal Security and Public Order Department to strengthen ongoing enforcement operations.
Port Klang MCBA commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the decision was reached following discussions with the department's deputy director Datuk Mohamad Suzrin Mohamad Rodhi, as well as other members of the joint task force.
"The decision to add them to the task force was unanimous, and they will be instrumental in our operations," he told reporters here today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two victims in Lebuh King law firm fire died of smoke inhalation
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Post-mortem examinations have confirmed that the two people who perished in a fire involving a two-storey shophouse in Lebuh King here died due to smoke inhalation.
Northeast district deputy police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said the victims comprised renowned veteran lawyer Datuk San Markan Ganapathi, 88, and Chandra Binwani Jiwatram, 60, a clerk at the firm.
"Both victims are confirmed to have died from asphyxia due to smoke inhalation," he said, adding that the post-mortem findings were completed following forensic examinations.
Yesterday, Penang Fire and Rescue Department director Mohamad Shoki Hamzah said the two victims who perished each suffered
30 per cent burns
.
San Markan was discovered near a window, seated on a chair, at 1.05pm.
Chandra was found later near a wall, beneath a pile of files, at 3.32pm.
Both victims were trapped inside the legal office on the top floor of the building following the blaze.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM orders swift response after religious school fire
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has instructed relevant agencies to expedite immediate action following a fire at SMU(A) Darul Ulum Muhammadiah in Kelantan.
He said the incident was a major test for the school community and stressed that urgent steps must be taken to ensure safety and recovery efforts are carried out swiftly.
"This is a major test for the SMU(A) Darul Ulum Muhammadiah community. I have instructed the Education Ministry, the Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU) of the Prime Minister's Department, and other relevant agencies to expedite decisive action.
"Our priority at this time is to ensure the safety of students and the continuity of learning that has been affected by the incident," he said in a Facebook post.
He said the Madani government would ensure that all necessary assistance is delivered without delay, while working closely with all parties to speed up recovery efforts.
He said student welfare remains a key priority for the government.
"As I have stressed before, the safety, comfort and welfare of children in their pursuit of education will always remain the government's priority," he said.
Three
school blocks
caught fire on Sunday, with no casualties reported.
Following this,
the MOE
approved RM100,000 allocation for cleaning and basic repair works, including electrical wiring and the restoration of water supply at the school.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US seeking 20-year pause of Iran uranium enrichment
Article:
WASHINGTON: The United States sought a 20-year suspension of Iran's uranium enrichment programme as part of a deal to end the war, according to media reports Monday, after Washington and Teheran failed to come to an agreement.
US President Donald Trump launched the war on Feb 28 under the pretext that Iran was developing an atomic bomb – which it denies – and vowed to never let the Islamic republic get its hands on a nuclear weapon.
Vice President JD Vance left negotiations with Iran over the weekend empty-handed, with sticking points including freeing up the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's nuclear programme.
Media outlets reported that Washington asked Teheran to agree not to enrich uranium for 20 years, citing officials close to the negotiations held in Islamabad on Saturday.
The 20-year pause would be accompanied by sanctions relief, the Wall Street Journal reported.
Iran in turn proposed to suspend its nuclear activity for five years, according to The New York Times.
The reported proposals would be a watered-down version of Trump's previous demands that Iran permanently give up its nuclear ambitions.
In 2018, Trump pulled out of what he called a "one-sided" pact that lifted sanctions on Iran in exchange for guarantees that Teheran would not make an atomic bomb.
Vance said that Washington had made its red lines clear in talks with Teheran, and that the "ball is in the Iranian court" now.
"There are two things in particular where the president of the United States really said we have no flexibility," Vance said on Monday.
"It's one thing for the Iranians to say that they're not going to have a nuclear weapon. It's another thing for us to put in place the mechanism to ensure that's not going to happen," he added.
Iran has previously ruled out restrictions on its right to enrich uranium in what it insists is a civilian nuclear program.
Earlier on Monday, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Vance had told him the removal of all of Iran's 60-percent enriched uranium from the country is a "central issue" for Trump.
The Washington delegation also wanted to ensure "that there is no further enrichment in the years ahead – even decades ahead – no enrichment within Iran," Netanyahu added.
Russia has offered to hold Iran's enriched uranium safely as part of any deal on Monday.
"The offer still stands, but has not been acted upon," Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov told reporters. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Most Malaysians happy at work, but stress remains concern
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Most Malaysian workers say they are happy at work, but rising stress and burnout suggest workplace wellbeing remains more fragile than headline figures indicate.
According to Jobstreet by Seek's latest Workplace Happiness Index, 70 per cent of workers report being somewhat or extremely happy in their jobs.
A similar proportion say they feel valued at work, while 68 per cent find their work fulfilling.
However, stress and burnout continue to shape the overall employee experience, with three in 10 workers either unhappy or neutral about their jobs.
The index is based on responses from about 1,000 employed individuals aged 18 to 64 in Malaysia, offering insights for employers, human resource leaders and policymakers.
The findings come against the backdrop of a relatively strong labour market, supported by steady job opportunities and low unemployment.
However, underlying pressures persist, with only 36 per cent of workers satisfied with their stress levels, while 41 per cent reported feeling burnt out or exhausted by their jobs.
According to the report, unhappy workers are twice as likely to experience burnout compared with those who are happy.
Among those unhappy at work, 71 per cent said better work-life balance would improve their happiness, making it the second most important factor after higher pay.
This underscores concerns that unmanaged stress and burnout could affect wellbeing, absenteeism and long-term productivity.
Despite these challenges, the report found workplace happiness is largely shaped by day-to-day fundamentals.
Workers reported the highest satisfaction with where they work (69 per cent), who they work with (65 per cent), and what they do daily (65 per cent).
The findings suggest predictable routines, supportive colleagues and meaningful roles remain central to positive workplace experiences.
While salary remains important in attracting and retaining talent, the report highlighted that purpose is increasingly key to sustaining workplace happiness.
Jobstreet by Seek Malaysia managing director Nicholas Lam said that while 70 per cent reporting happiness is encouraging, a significant group still feel disengaged, burnt out or unsupported.
He said purpose has a strong link to workplace happiness, with 64 per cent of workers satisfied with the sense of purpose in their jobs.
"Workplace happiness in Malaysia is a shared responsibility. When employers invest in purpose-driven roles, supportive cultures and listen to the diverse needs of their workforce, the impact is tangible, translating into higher engagement, productivity and retention.
"Ultimately, building happier workplaces is not just good for workers, it is essential for long-term business success," he said in a statement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gen Z workers report lower happiness, higher stress
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Gen Z workers in Malaysia report lower workplace happiness and higher stress levels compared with older generations, highlighting growing pressures among early-career employees.
According to Jobstreet by Seek's Workplace Happiness Index, Gen Z employees are more likely to experience stress as they navigate early career challenges, skill development and the need to prove themselves.
They are also more likely to say that reducing stress would significantly improve their happiness, at 40 per cent, compared with 35 per cent for millennials and 27 per cent for Gen X, the report said.
Gen Z refers to those born between 1997 and 2012, now aged between 14 and 29.
The index is based on responses from about 1,000 employed individuals aged 18 to 64 in Malaysia, offering insights for employers, human resource leaders and policymakers.
While most Malaysian workers report being happy at work, rising stress and burnout suggest workplace wellbeing remains more fragile than headline figures indicate.
The report also found variations across industries.
Professional services and construction recorded the highest happiness levels at 78 per cent each, while the public sector (62 per cent), industrial sector (61 per cent), and retail, hospitality and sports (55 per cent) reported lower levels.
To improve workplace happiness, Jobstreet by Seek said employers should focus on strengthening employees' sense of purpose, building supportive workplace cultures, and adopting more tailored approaches to different generational needs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Xi meets Spain PM in China as Madrid seeks stronger ties
Article:
BEIJING: China's President Xi Jinping met Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez in Beijing on Tuesday, according to Chinese state media, with the visit centred on strengthening economic ties.
The meeting of the two leaders comes on the second day of Sanchez's visit as he seeks to position Spain as a bridge between Beijing and the European Union, whose relations with the United States are under strain.
"On the morning of April 14, President Xi Jinping met with Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez, who is on an official visit to China, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing," state broadcaster CCTV reported.
Sanchez is seeking to strengthen economic ties with the world's second-largest economy, but called China's trade imbalance with the EU "unsustainable" on Monday.
The Spanish prime minister is on his fourth visit to China in four years and follows a steady flow of Western leaders visiting Beijing in recent months as President Donald Trump's tariffs and unpredictable foreign policy have rattled the US's traditional allies. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two killed as chicken-laden lorry overturns on WCE near Sitiawan
Article:
BERUAS: Two men were killed after a lorry carrying chickens skidded and overturned at Km194.7 of the West Coast Expressway (WCE) near Sitiawan today.
Perak Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the department received a distress call at 10.11am, and a team of firefighters from Sitiawan rushed to the scene.
"The accident involved a lorry transporting chickens, which is believed to have lost control before overturning onto the road shoulder.
"Two male victims were thrown out of the vehicle and became trapped beside the lorry," he said.
Sabarodzi said one of the victims was a 45-year-old local man, while the other was a Vietnamese national believed to be in his 30s.
"Both victims were pronounced dead at the scene by medical personnel," he said, adding that firefighters used rescue tools to extricate the victims before handing their remains over to police for further action.
He said a total of seven personnel and one fire engine were deployed for the operation.
"The operation was brought under control at 11.10am and fully concluded at 11.32am," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mideast war revs up electric car demand in Asia
Article:
HANOI: Electric vehicle sales have jumped in Southeast Asia as cost-conscious buyers have poured into dealerships looking to dodge the fuel price spikes driven by the Middle East war.
Asian nations have been particularly hard hit due to a sharp fall in the crude shipments they rely on -- and have few alternatives to replace them.
Yet the energy crisis has been a windfall for Vietnam's leading electric vehicle maker Vinfast as well as Chinese manufacturers.
Vietnamese office worker Do Thi Lan explained the simple math of the cars' appeal at a Vinfast showroom in Hanoi.
"We have to calculate our monthly expenses, as the money we spend on petroleum has been on the rise," she said.
She said her family owns a car that runs on petrol but was considering buying an electric vehicle to save money.
Dao Thi Hue, also at the showroom, was looking to go electric too.
"Driving an EV is so much better than driving a petroleum vehicle, in terms of costs and also in terms of saving fuel, queuing to fill up," the school teacher said.
Crude oil prices have soared by around 50 per cent since the start of the Middle East war and again exceeded US$100 per barrel on Monday, driving up the cost at the pump.
Vinfast, listed on the Nasdaq, saw a 127 per cent surge in annual sales in Vietnam in March, reaching 27,600 cars.
About 40 per cent of cars sold in Vietnam in 2025 were electric, but the trend has been accelerating.
"At this point in time, clients consider fuel costs a lot when making a decision on which cars to buy," said Pham Minh Hai, deputy head of sales at a Vinfast showroom.
"In March we sold 300-400 cars," he said, noting that the showroom normally sells between 200 and 250 cars a month.
Hai said more than 50 per cent of his clients changed from petroleum to electric cars last month, while the number of customers at the showroom was up by around 30 per cent.
He added that opening hours had been extended to deal with the rush.
Outside Vietnam, Chinese manufacturers specialising in electric vehicles, particularly Tesla's main rival BYD, are booming.
- 'Punished by gas prices' -
At the Bangkok Auto Show earlier this month, BYD secured the most orders of any manufacturer, surpassing Japan's Toyota for the first time.
"I drive a lot, nearly 100 kilometres (60 miles) a day... with the current fuel situation and no idea how long it will last, it's become a major factor pushing me to make the switch," said Pleng Nawintham, a 36-year-old pharmacist from Thailand.
BYD was also seeing increased sales in the Philippines.
Mae Anne Clarisse Bacquiano, manager of a BYD showroom in the suburbs of Manila, said foot traffic at the dealership was "at another level".
"It was all because of the rise in fuel prices," she said. "Earlier today, I had a customer, a doctor who was ranting to me about how he is being punished by gas prices... He was in a hurry to go full electric. There'd be a huge difference in expenses."
She added that all of her stock for the month had already been reserved by buyers.
"I don't expect the gas (prices) to go back down over the next couple of months," said Arlone Abello, an entrepreneur who was browsing BYD models at the showroom.
As BYD sales decline in China due to fierce local competition, the manufacturer hopes to gain international momentum.
The company told analysts that it now expects to exceed 1.5 million exported vehicles in 2026, well above the 1.3 million target announced in January.
- Structural change -
Exports of Chinese electric vehicles -- for which Southeast Asia is a major market -- doubled in March, compared to the same month last year across all manufacturers, according to the industry association CPCA.
Economic factors are at the forefront of the increased demand for greener vehicles.
"You have the individual consumer response to what they are seeing in terms of the price of petrol or diesel suddenly surge," said Euan Graham, an electricity and data analyst at energy think tank Ember.
The installation of charging stations in the region is also growing rapidly.
Jakarta promised last week to take "more serious steps to accelerate the development of a national electric vehicle ecosystem" to combat its "high level of energy consumption".
Electric vehicles are gaining momentum beyond Southeast Asia.
"There are signs that global demand has already picked up substantially," Capital Economics said, adding that registrations of electric vehicles in Japan, South Korea, and New Zealand more than doubled in March, and rose by over 50 per cent in India, Australia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: '10pct return in 30 minutes' lure leaves man RM427,000 poorer
Article:
KUANTAN: A salesman's decision to sell a piece of land in pursuit of higher returns ended in a devastating loss when he was tricked out of RM427,000 in a dubious investment scheme.
The 44-year-old private sector employee was lured by promises of 10 per cent returns within 30 minutes of investment through the scheme.
Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the victim was contacted by an unknown individual via WhatsApp on Dec 17 last year, inviting him to invest in a "Growth Investment" scheme.
"The victim was attracted to the scheme, which promised a 10 per cent return within 30 minutes.
"He was then added to a WhatsApp group chat and instructed to download an investment application before registering as a member.
"The man made 11 transactions totalling RM427,000 into two separate bank accounts between Feb 10 and April 3.
"He believed the scheme was genuine after receiving RM23,000 in profits, but there have been no updates since then," he said in a statement today.
He said the victim lodged a police report at the Kuantan district police headquarters yesterday after realising that he had been cheated.
"The victim claimed he used his personal savings and proceeds from the sale of a piece of land to invest in the scheme," he said.
Yahaya reminded the public to verify bank account numbers via https://semakmule.rmp.gov.my/
before making any transactions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No medications were administered to Zara on the way to hospital
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: No medications, including the anti-epileptic drug, phenytoin, were given to the late Zara Qairina Mahathir before she arrived at the Queen Elizabeth Hospital (QEH), the coroner's court was told.
Medical assistant Mohd Faiszal Md Saimi from Kinarut Health Clinic, testified that no drugs were administered during the initial emergency treatment until his team handed Zara Qairina over to personnel at QEH.
"The priority in early emergency treatment for unconscious trauma patients, suspected of having head injuries, is the management of the airway, control of bleeding and circulation, instead of administering medications.
"Administering medication may worsen the victim's condition, such as causing a drop in blood pressure or increased bleeding," Mohd Faiszal, the 69th witness, told coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan.
On July 16 at about 3.23am, Faiszal responded to an emergency call and arrived at 3.45am at Zara Qairina's school dormitory at SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha.
Replying to a question from coordinating officer Sofia Samayan, the 40-year-old said his role as a medical assistant prevented him from prescribing and administering drugs.
Mohd Faiszal said drugs he could prescribe include paracetamol, aspirin, glyceryl trinitrate, oral rehydration salts, and infusion fluids such as 0.9 per cent normal saline.
He also denied administering phenytoin, which is an anti-epileptic drug. The witness said Zara Qairina was unresponsive and did not display any signs of having a seizure.
Questioned further by lawyer Shahlan Jufri about phenytoin, Mohd Faiszal said the drug similar to diazepam, is a controlled medicine that can only be prescribed by medical officers.
"Phenytoin, according to the guidelines, is kept at health clinics.
"But for seizure cases, the first choice is diazepam. Phenytoin is the last-line drug for uncontrolled seizure cases, such as when the patient remains in a state of seizure for over five minutes or has repetitive seizures despite being given diazepam."
The witness said the public could also buy phenytoin in tablet or capsule form from pharmacies with a prescription from medical officers or specialists.
Shahlan is representing Zara Qairina's mother in the inquest.
The
post-mortem toxicology report
for Zara Qairina indicated that traces of phenytoin were found in her kidney.
In previous proceedings, neither the QEH Emergency and Trauma Department medical officer nor the neurosurgeon who attended to Zara Qairina testified to administering phenytoin
during their respective shifts.
Zara Qairina, 13, was found unconscious on the ground floor of her hostel before being pronounced dead the following day, on July 17, 2025, at the hospital.
The inquest will resume on Thursday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PKR slams Trump's retort to Pope's plea for peace
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: PKR's International Affairs Bureau has expressed its solidarity with Pope Leo XIV, commending his principled stand against rising military tensions involving the United States and Iran.
The bureau's chairman, Dr Maszlee Malik, praised the Pope's call for restraint and the sanctity of human life amid the beating of the drums of war.
"Keadilan views with contempt the retort issued by US President Donald Trump against the leader of the Catholic Church.
"For a political leader to dismiss the Holy Father as 'weak on crime' and 'terrible for foreign policy' simply for advocating dialogue is the height of diplomatic indecency," Maszlee said in a statement today.
He added that Trump's demand for the Church to confine its views to his political agenda was an assault on religious freedom and moral independence.
The bureau has consistently condemned the militarism of the US administration, describing it as a violation of international law and the UN Charter.
Maszlee noted that the administration's penchant for unilateral strikes and the use of religious imagery to depict the president as a Christ-like figure exposed a "delusion of omnipotence".
He said the Pope had rightly reminded the global community that religion should not be used to justify war, stating that his refusal to be intimidated by the US administration embodied moral courage.
"We echo the sentiments of global leaders and Catholic communities who see President Trump's reaction as an insult not only to 1.3 billion Catholics but to all people of conscience who value peace," he said.
Keadilan also called on the international community to support the Vatican's plea for immediate de-escalation and a return to multilateral diplomacy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCBA logs 231 enforcement cases involving RM36.2mil in goods
Article:
PORT KLANG: The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) has taken enforcement action on 231 containers between January and March this year, involving goods valued at RM36.2 million.
Port Klang MCBA commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the cases stemmed from investigations conducted by multiple agencies, including Maqis, the Health Ministry and the Customs Department.
"Overall, enforcement actions taken by MCBA from January to March involved 231 containers, with a total value of RM36,243,214.11," he said at a press conference today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Over 400,000 ANPR transactions recorded since January, says Nanta
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: More than 400,000 transactions have been recorded through the Automated Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) system since its pilot rollout in January.
Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi said the figure reflects growing public acceptance of the system, which is part of the government's broader push towards a seamless toll collection mechanism.
"So far, we have recorded over 400,000 transactions since January 2026. This is a positive indicator that the platform is gaining traction," he said at a press conference after attending the Works Ministry's monthly assembly.
The pilot project covers the Hutan Kampung–Sungai Dua stretch, involving nine toll plazas, namely, Hutan Kampung, Alor Star Utara, Alor Star Selatan, Pendang, Gurun, Sungai Petani Utara, Sungai Petani Selatan, Bertam and Sungai Dua.
Nanta said the ANPR system is currently operational at nine toll plazas nationwide, covering 78 lanes.
He added that about 28,000 users have registered on the JomGo platform, which was developed by Plus Malaysia Bhd in collaboration with other highway concessionaires.
"The number of users and transactions shows that the system is functioning well and can be further expanded," he said.
Nanta added that there have been no major technical issues reported since the system's introduction.
"From the feedback received, operations have been smooth, and the system is performing as expected," he said.
The ANPR system allows toll payments to be made automatically by detecting vehicle number plates, eliminating the need for physical payment methods.
It forms part of Malaysia's transition towards the Multi-Lane Free Flow (MLFF) system, which is targeted for full implementation by 2027.
Nanta said the government would continue to monitor the system's performance and leave further expansion plans to the concessionaires involved.
"We will allow the operators to carry out their plans in line with the overall target of implementing MLFF by 2027," he said.
During the pilot phase, physical barriers will remain in place, and users are advised to maintain speeds of 20 to 25km/h when passing through toll plazas.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCBA seizes RM1 million worth of frozen meat over falsified documents
Article:
PORT KLANG: The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) has seized seven containers of frozen meat worth more than RM1 million following the discovery of suspected falsified import documents.
Port Klang MCBA commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the seizure at North Port on April 9 was based on a public tip-off.
The operation involved six containers of frozen chicken and one container of frozen duck, weighing a total of 187,800kg.
"The total estimated value of the seized goods is RM1,062,048. Initial checks found discrepancies in the documentation submitted, including invoices and packing lists that did not match the Notice of Arrival," he said at a press conference today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: First batch of 15 e-waste containers shipped out of Malaysia
Article:
PORT KLANG: Malaysian authorities have shipped out the first batch of 15 containers of illegal electronic waste (e-waste), marking a key milestone in ongoing enforcement efforts at Port Klang.
Port Klang Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the containers were transported via Westports at 8am today as part of coordinated operations under Op Green Shield.
"Alhamdulillah, today 15 e-waste containers have been shipped out.
"This is the result of close coordination between the Joint Task Force comprising MCBA, Customs Department, the Department of Environment, SIRIM and the Port Klang Authority," he told reporters here today.
The operation was carried out under Op Green Shield, which was established on Jan 27 this year to strengthen monitoring, inspections and enforcement actions, as well as to streamline the re-export of illegal e-waste to its country of origin in accordance with existing laws and international conventions.
Nik Ezanee said the milestone comes amid a broader enforcement effort, where 254 containers confirmed to contain e-waste have been detained following inspections conducted under multiple phases of the operation.
He added that, to date, a total of 803 containers have been subjected to enforcement action for removal from the country, reflecting the scale of the ongoing crackdown.
"This is a milestone for us. It shows that when all agencies work together with a shared objective, we can move forward effectively in addressing illegal e-waste imports," he said.
However, he cautioned that the more challenging phase lies ahead, particularly in sustaining coordination and clearing the remaining backlog of containers.
"The main challenge is yet to come. Maintaining this level of cooperation and momentum will be crucial in ensuring all remaining containers are successfully repatriated," he said.
Nik Ezanee also expressed appreciation to shipping liners for facilitating the process, noting that their cooperation was key to ensuring smooth repatriation.
Under Op Green Shield, authorities have intensified inspections of containers suspected of carrying prohibited electronic waste, in line with the absolute ban on e-waste imports enforced through the Customs (Prohibition of Imports) Order.
The operation forms part of ongoing efforts to ensure Malaysia does not become a dumping ground for illegal e-waste, while strengthening enforcement at entry points to safeguard environmental sustainability and national sovereignty.
To date, MCBA has seized over 6,200 tonnes of e-waste detected through Op Green Shield at ports here.
In one of its most recent operations on April 8, MCBA Port Klang seized more than 210 tonnes of electronic and scheduled waste.
The operation, led by Port Klang MCBA deputy commander Wan Fazli Wan Hamat, involved 18 officers from the participating agencies.
Preliminary investigations found nine containers carrying electronic waste (SW110) weighing 184,627kg, while one container contained scheduled waste (SW103) weighing 26,200kg.
The total weight of seized waste amounted to 210,827kg.
Five containers containing plastic and metal scrap were handed to SIRIM for further action.
Authorities also uncovered false declarations submitted by two companies on K1 forms, leading to enforcement action and referral to Customs for follow-up.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mida Singapore sees growing investment into Johor
Article:
SINGAPORE: Johor continues to see a good traction of investment coming from Singapore, providing a seamless journey for investors looking to expand their businesses in the southern state, said the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida).
MIDA Singapore director Vinothan Tulisinathzan said that among the major industries attracting investment from Singapore are the digital economy, semiconductor, and food and beverages (F&B) businesses.
He noted that investment opportunities in the digital economy within Johor fell into two main sections -- digital technology providers and digital infrastructure providers.
"In the digital technology provider (section), you have blockchains, artificial intelligence (AI), things like that, but you can see more traction in the digital infrastructure provider, which is the data centre players.
"Johor has an amazing traction in attracting data centres, and we are being more selective in data centres to be more AI, energy efficient and so forth," he said.
He was speaking during a roundtable discussion entitled "How Johor and Singapore are reshaping cross-border investment" organised by Singapore Press Club and the Johor Economic, Tourism and Cultural Office Singapore (Jetco) on Monday.
According to Vinothan, Mida also saw rising interest from Singaporean semiconductor players entering Johor, both from the back-end and front-end firms.
"Most importantly, we can see a good traction of the Singapore-based companies that are involved in the supply chain, and they could be the tier one or tier two," he added.
Additionally, the agency witnessed growing interest among F&B operators in expanding their operations to Johor Bahru amid the rising cost of doing business.
"We have a company that has headquarters in Singapore, but it sets up a central kitchen in Johor, and it has a halal operation, which gives the company a competitive edge to export its products in the region and even in the Middle East," he said.
Also present at the event were Malaysian High Commissioner to Singapore Datuk Azfar Mohamad Mustafar and Jetco executive chairman Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Medicine supply stable until June, public told to avoid stockpiling
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The supply of medicine in the country is expected to remain stable at least until the end of June, with the public advised against panic buying or stockpiling.
Health Ministry Pharmacy Practice and Development Division senior principal assistant director Muhammad Md Zin said stocks at government healthcare facilities are continuously monitored to ensure uninterrupted supply.
He cautioned that excessive stockpiling was impractical as medicines have expiry dates and require specific storage conditions.
"Keeping excessive supplies increases the risk of using damaged or expired medicines, which can be harmful. It is best to manage medication wisely, avoid bulk purchases and seek advice from healthcare professionals," he said.
Muhammad was speaking on the Apa Khabar Malaysia programme on Bernama TV today, where he discussed the impact of the global energy crisis on domestic medicine supplies.
He advised patients with chronic conditions, such as hypertension and diabetes mellitus, not to alter or ration their prescribed medication without professional guidance.
"There is no concept of saving money by reducing dosage. Continue taking medication as prescribed. Any adjustments must be made in consultation with medical experts to avoid health risks," he stressed.
Muhammad further warned against purchasing medicines indiscriminately, especially through online platforms, without verifying their authenticity and registration with the Health Ministry.
He said consumers can check the legitimacy of medicines through the National Pharmaceutical Regulatory Agency website or by consulting healthcare professionals.
"Buying medicines without a prescription also carries the risk of incorrect dosage, which can be dangerous," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Netizens back public lesson as illegally parked car in Kajang gets 'rubbish treatment'
Article:
KAJANG: A Facebook post showing a car obstructing a shop lot road in Sepakat Indah 2 has gone viral, with many netizens expressing support with the person who left rubbish on the vehicle.
The post features images of a grey Proton Saga parked in the middle of a narrow access road, with multiple garbage bags placed on its roof and around the vehicle.
The obstruction appeared to disrupt traffic flow, forcing other road users to carefully manoeuvre past the car.
According to the caption accompanying the post, the vehicle owner had allegedly left the car in the middle of the road while dining nearby, blocking access for others.
Many commenters appeared to support the retaliatory act, viewing it as a justified response to inconsiderate parking.
"This is the right way to teach those who behave irresponsibly," one Facebook user @CikAsmaraIsabelle said.
Another user @Rahim Bakar commented, "Good way to deal with drivers who park without thinking of others."
The incident has sparked online debate, with some arguing that such reactions reflect growing frustration over irresponsible behaviour, while others cautioned against taking matters into one's own hands.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MBSB IB sees Brent, CPO holding above support
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MBSB Investment Bank Bhd expects benchmark Brent crude oil and crude palm oil prices to remain above support levels and potentially re-test resistance at US$114 per barrel and RM4,890 per tonne, respectively, in the near term.
In a research note today, it said the outlook is driven mainly by the United States military's planned blockade of Iranian ports from April 13.
"This (blockade), and any further escalation, would likely disrupt the market. We therefore recommend taking long positions in crude oil and other commodity-related stocks, supported by fundamental upside," it said.
RHB Investment Bank Bhd said in a more adverse scenario where the conflict persists or escalates, oil prices could rise sharply, potentially reaching US$140 to US$150 per barrel, depending on the severity and duration of supply disruptions.
"We do not expect oil prices to revert to pre-conflict levels immediately, as restart lags, logistical frictions and lingering risk premiums could keep prices elevated in the near term.
"However, as supply chains stabilise, we expect prices to settle around the mid-US$80 per barrel level before trending lower towards our medium-term assumptions," it said.
RHB raised its average Brent crude oil forecast for 2026 to US$82.50 per barrel from US$62 previously, and introduced a 2027 forecast of US$72 per barrel.
Year to date, Brent has averaged about US$80.50 per barrel, with first-quarter 2026 averaging US$77.80 per barrel, reflecting heightened geopolitical tensions.
Meanwhile, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd said that while the duration of the war remains uncertain, it expects Brent prices to stay elevated for about five months, similar to the period following the outbreak of the Russia-Ukraine war in 2022.
"We revise our Brent assumptions for 2026 and 2027 to US$90 per barrel and US$75 per barrel, from US$70 and US$65 previously," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Phillip Capital initiates Keyfield with 'Buy', TP RM1.70
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Phillip Research Sdn Bhd (Phillip Capital) has initiated coverage on Keyfield International Bhd with a "Buy" call and a target price of RM1.70, citing strong earnings growth over the next three years.
The firm projects a net profit compound annual growth rate of 17 per cent over the period, driven by fleet expansion, stronger margins and improved operational performance.
Keyfield recently expanded its fleet with three vessels, namely Keyfield Gratitude, Keyfield Blessing and Keyfield Harmony, which are expected to contribute a full year of earnings in 2026.
Phillip Capital said growth momentum will be supported by the newbuild vessels, comprising one anchor handling tug supply vessel and two anchor handling tug supply-equivalent assets, scheduled for delivery between 2027 and 2028.
The firm also highlighted Keyfield's expansion into the Middle East following its 65 per cent acquisition of Keyfield Biham.
The unit has secured three contracts since December 2025, improving earnings visibility and supporting higher fleet deployment.
Utilisation is projected to rise to 70 per cent in 2026, 72 per cent in 2027 and 74 per cent in 2028, from 63 per cent in 2025.
Phillip Capital said rising utilisation, coupled with the deployment of newer, higher-capability vessels commanding better charter rates, is expected to lift net profit margin to 31 per cent from 25 per cent.
Keyfield is trading at about eight times forward 2027 earnings, which could support a potential re-rating as sector sentiment improves, the firm said.
Key risks include weaker demand for offshore support vessels, higher-than-expected operating costs and a sharp decline in global crude oil prices.
Keyfield's competitive position is supported by its relatively young fleet, with an average age of nine years compared with the industry average of 12 years.
The group operates 14 vessels, including anchor handling tug supply vessels, platform supply vessels, accommodation work barges and a cable-laying barge.
As a Petronas-licensed operator, Keyfield also has access to tender pipelines from Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) and petroleum arrangement contractors, supporting recurring contract flows and utilisation stability.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bus driver in viral video is married, say police [WATCH]
Article:
JASIN: Police have confirmed that the express bus driver seen in a viral video driving with a woman seated on his lap is married.
District police chief Superintendent Lee Robert said the 36-year-old man is believed to reside in Johor, while the identity of the woman has yet to be established.
He said police have not, at this stage, obtained further information regarding the relationship between the driver and the woman involved in the incident.
"Police view the matter seriously as it involves a public service vehicle carrying passengers.
"We also urge the express bus driver and the woman featured in the video recording to come forward immediately to the Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department at the Jasin district police headquarters to assist in investigations," he said in a statement here today.
Earlier, the media reported that
police were tracking down the bus driver
who was recorded driving with a woman on his lap, with the video going viral on social media since yesterday.
Lee said preliminary investigations found that the incident occurred at about 7.30pm on April 12 at the southbound slip road to the Bemban Rest and Service (R&R) area.
Those with information on the incident are urged to contact investigating officer Inspector Marwan Shafiq Mohd Latfi at 011-1112 7533 or visit the nearest police station.
"The case is being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Late-night delivery turns touching as customer gives duit raya to disabled rider [WATCH]
Article:
MACHANG: A late-night food delivery has captured hearts online after a customer shared a brief but meaningful encounter with a disabled rider.
The woman said the man, known on TikTok as @zaik.grabrider94, was someone who often delivered her orders, describing him as hardworking and determined despite his condition.
During the exchange, she learned that he had yet to eat, even as he continued working late into the night.
Moved by his perseverance, she gave him duit raya as a token of appreciation.
The moment resonated with many, with some expressing admiration and offering prayers for the rider.
